data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace operates in Space & Aerospace at the public market stage. The applicable peer universe comprises 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M), alongside a broader 5-ticker public comp set including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL.

Peer-median valuation anchoring was the applicable triangulation method, yielding a median implied value of $7.0B across the 3-company cohort. The peer range spans $900M to $37.0B, reflecting material dispersion within the sector. Revenue-based approaches using the public Space & Aerospace P/S median of 64.2x could not be applied in the absence of disclosed target revenue figures, a common constraint for private-stage analysis in this sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered at $7.0B. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits within the lower portion of this range, approximately 10% below the peer median. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to public peers, capital efficiency in the capital-intensive launch and satellite operations segments, and near-term commercialization timing. The concentration of valuation support on a single method (peer-median) suggests reliance on comparable company multiples rather than cash flow or market-based approaches; additional data on Firefly's revenue run-rate would enable P/S validation.
